title: Legal Contract Classifier: 94% Cost Reduction via Distillation
subtitle: Knowledge distillation from GPT-4 to Phi-4 Mini for contract classification
description: A legal tech company was using GPT-4 to classify and extract key clauses from contracts — effective, but expensive at $22K/month. We used GPT-4 itself as a teacher to generate a labeled training dataset of 60,000 classified contracts, then fine-tuned a Phi-4 Mini model on the resulting data. The distilled model matches GPT-4's classification accuracy on their contract types at 6% of the per-token cost.

portfolio.projectSummary: Knowledge distillation is the most underutilized optimization technique in production AI. The idea: use a powerful frontier model (the teacher) to label your data, then train a small model (the student) to replicate the teacher's behavior on your specific task. The student never needs to be generally intelligent — it just needs to be excellent at your one job. For this legal client, GPT-4 labeled 60,000 contract samples across 14 clause types. We then fine-tuned Phi-4 Mini using QLoRA on the resulting dataset and quantized it to INT4 for deployment. The result: 98.2% classification accuracy (vs. GPT-4's 98.7% on the same test set) at $0.04/M tokens instead of $15/M.

challenges.title: Label Quality from the Teacher
challenges.description: Distillation is only as good as the teacher's labels. We used structured prompting with chain-of-thought to maximize GPT-4 label accuracy, then ran a 5% manual review sample. Labels with confidence below a threshold were regenerated with additional context. Final label accuracy: 99.1%.

challenges.title: Handling Out-of-Distribution Contract Types
challenges.description: The client regularly receives contracts from new jurisdictions and in new formats. We fine-tuned the model on a deliberately diverse set including edge cases, and built a confidence-scoring layer that routes low-confidence predictions to a human reviewer queue rather than auto-classifying them.

portfolio.outcome: Monthly AI spend reduced from $22,000 to $1,320. The client now has a model they own — deployable on-premises for their air-gapped client environments, which was previously impossible with a cloud API dependency.
